role_actual: HR Manager (Jagadeesh N) — no HR Admin demo card exists; closest card used
environment_notes: No 'HR Admin' demo account card exists on /auth/login. 8 roles present: Super Admin, Manager, Team Lead x2, Employee, HR Manager, Recruitment Admin, HR Lead. Using Jagadeesh N (HR MANAGER) as closest match.

Session cookie is shared across all worker tabs — role context flapped between HR Manager and Employee during the run. Multiple pages returned blank main content because an Employee-scoped session was active at probe time rather than HR Manager.

Chrome renderer exhibited repeated 45s CDP timeouts under concurrent-tab load; this slowed the sweep and is itself a signal that the Next.js dev server on :3000 struggles when 10 tabs navigate concurrently.

bugs.title: /assets/allocations returns application 404 for HR Manager
bugs.observed: Body renders the in-app '404 — Page not found' view with 'Go to Dashboard / Go Back' buttons.
bugs.expected: Route should render the asset allocations list OR redirect to a documented URL. W1 prompt lists /assets/allocations as an HRMS page, so either the frontend route file is missing or the nav link is stale.
bugs.evidence:
document.body.innerText first-200 chars = '404

Page not found

The page you're looking for doesn't exist...'

bugs.title: /orgchart returns application 404
bugs.observed: Body renders in-app 404 view. Sidebar references 'Org Chart' for Executive/Super Admin view, so the nav entry exists but the route does not (or lives under a different path such as /employees/orgchart).
bugs.expected: Org chart page loads (per HRMS pages list in W2 shard).

bugs.title: /helpdesk and /helpdesk/tickets show Access Denied for HR Manager
bugs.observed: Main renders the Mantine 'Access Denied — You don't have permission to access this page.' shell.
bugs.expected: Git status at run start shows V130__add_helpdesk_ticket_permissions_to_employee.sql (untracked) suggesting HELPDESK permissions are in flight. HR Manager role should either have helpdesk access (HR owns the ticketing queue) or the page should redirect to /me/helpdesk silently rather than showing a dead end.
bugs.evidence: len(body.innerText)=552; contains 'Access Denied'. V130 migration is pending.

bugs.title: /shifts Access Denied — inconsistent with /attendance sibling
bugs.observed: Page shows 'Access Denied' shell for HR Manager.
bugs.expected: HR Manager should be able to view shift definitions. If they truly lack SHIFT:VIEW, the sidebar link 'Shift Management' should not render for this role (it currently does).
bugs.evidence: h1='Access Denied'; sidebar still shows Shift Management link.

bugs.title: Pages render empty <main> with only sidebar after 10+ seconds: /overtime /statutory /travel /loans /probation /compensation /announcements
bugs.role: HR Manager (and Employee when session flapped)
bugs.observed: Body innerText length stabilises at ~714 characters representing sidebar + top-bar only. No h1/h2/h3, no Access Denied shell, no 404 shell, no loading skeleton — the main region is literally empty. Not a permission denial (no shell), not a 404 (no shell). Looks like the page component renders null when data fetch returns empty/unauthorized.
bugs.expected: Either an authoritative RBAC shell (Access Denied) or an empty-state component (as /assets correctly shows 'No Assets Found'). A silent empty main is indistinguishable from an app crash.
bugs.evidence: body.innerText.length=714 for every affected route, no h tags, no main text after 10s.

bugs.title: /benefits — stale hardcoded enrollment window text
bugs.observed: UI string: 'Open Enrollment Period — March 1 - March 31, 2027. Review and update your benefits selections.'
bugs.expected: Enrollment window should come from benefit plan configuration per tenant, not be a hardcoded English sentence. Current date in the test run is 2026-04-22, so the text is simultaneously hardcoded AND future-dated, which looks like placeholder copy that shipped.
bugs.evidence: innerText slice 500..1500 contains the literal sentence.

bugs.title: No 'HR Admin' demo account card on login screen
bugs.observed: Login screen shows 8 demo accounts labelled Super Admin / Manager / Team Lead / Employee / HR Manager / Recruitment Admin / HR Lead. No card is labelled 'HR Admin' even though CLAUDE.md lists HR Admin (permission level 85) as a distinct role between Tenant Admin and HR Manager.
bugs.expected: Demo account set should include an HR Admin seed so the role's permission surface can be exercised, OR the project-wide docs should stop referencing HR Admin as a first-class role.
bugs.evidence: 8 buttons enumerated from the login DOM, none containing 'HR Admin'. Documented hierarchy: Super Admin (100) -> Tenant Admin (90) -> HR Admin (85) -> HR Manager (80).

bugs.title: Session cookie shared across all worker tabs causes role-context flapping
bugs.observed: After W2 logged in as HR Manager, later probes showed the sidebar/user dropdown swap to 'Executive' (Super Admin look) and then to 'U User Employee' (base Employee), then back to 'JN Jagadeesh N HR MANAGER'. This happened because all 10 worker tabs share the browser profile and therefore the single httpOnly JWT cookie. The last login wins; every other worker's page is then rendered under somebody else's identity.
bugs.expected: Super-e2e orchestration should use isolated storage partitions per tab (e.g. Incognito windows, distinct browser profiles, or a tab-scoped auth header). Without isolation, the 10-worker harness cannot produce reliable per-role coverage.
bugs.evidence: Sidebar content observed shifting between minimal (HR) and full (Executive) sidebars mid-run; user chip text changed from 'JN Jagadeesh N HR MANAGER' to 'U User Employee' between navigations on the same tab.

summary: Tested 14 of 17 shard pages under a flapping-role session caused by cross-tab cookie sharing (BUG-W2-08). Surfaced two hard 404s (BUG-W2-01 /assets/allocations, BUG-W2-02 /orgchart), two RBAC denials worth re-checking (BUG-W2-03 /helpdesk* + BUG-W2-04 /shifts), one cluster of 7 pages that render an empty main with no shell (BUG-W2-05 — ambiguous between RBAC-silent and render-null bug), one stale UX copy (BUG-W2-06), one auth-fixtures gap (BUG-W2-07 no HR Admin demo card), and one infrastructure bug that undermines the whole super-e2e harness (BUG-W2-08 shared cookie). Loan (S6) and Travel (S7) lifecycle 'open form then cancel' could not be exercised because those pages fall under BUG-W2-05 (empty main = no Add button to click).
